overview: Activity over this period centered on two substantial infrastructure series: a 14-patch effort by Ihor Solodrai to have resolve_btfids emit BTF decl tags for kfuncs at build time, and a 6-patch v3 series from Emil Tsalapatis to minimize boilerplate annotations in BPF arena programs by leveraging BTF type tags. Daniel Borkmann contributed a second revision of gen_loader hardening fixes, and Tejun Heo landed a concurrency correctness fix for arena page allocation.

patches.title: selftests/bpf: libarena: Add "arena" BTF type tag to __arena qualifier
patches.author: Emil Tsalapatis
patches.summary: This is the first patch of a 6-part v3 series titled "Minimize annotations for arena programs." It encodes the "arena" semantic directly into the BTF type tag attached to the __arena qualifier so the verifier can identify arena pointers without extra per-argument annotations. Paired with subsequent patches that teach the verifier to parse these tags and allow subprograms to return arena pointers, the series significantly reduces the annotation burden for arena-heavy BPF programs. The selftests are updated throughout to drop the now-redundant __arg_arena annotations.

patches.title: verifier: parse BTF type tags for function arguments
patches.author: Emil Tsalapatis
patches.summary: Extends the BPF verifier to inspect BTF decl/type tags attached to function argument types, enabling tag-driven argument classification without explicit user-space annotations. This is the core verifier change in Emil's arena annotation minimization series: the verifier can now infer that a parameter carries arena semantics from the "arena" type tag alone. The change is backward compatible since existing programs without the tag continue to use the __arg_arena annotation path.

patches.title: bpf: Allow subprogs to return arena pointers
patches.author: Emil Tsalapatis
patches.summary: Teaches the verifier to accept arena-typed return values from BPF subprograms, completing the type-tag-based arena pointer tracking introduced earlier in the series. Previously, returning an arena pointer from a helper or subprog required workarounds; now the verifier propagates the arena type through function return values automatically. This unlocks cleaner factoring of arena-based BPF programs and is validated by the new selftests added in later patches of the series.

patches.title: tools/bpf: Sync btf_ids.h to tools
patches.author: Ihor Solodrai
patches.summary: First patch of Ihor Solodrai's 14-patch series "resolve_btfids: Implement BTF tags emission for kfuncs." It synchronizes the kernel's btf_ids.h header into the tools/ mirror so that resolve_btfids can access up-to-date KF_* flag definitions. The series as a whole moves kfunc metadata (bpf_kfunc, KF_FASTCALL, KF_ARENA_*) from pahole-driven flags into proper BTF decl tags emitted directly by the resolve_btfids build tool, eliminating the need for special pahole flags and making kfunc annotations self-contained in the BTF blob.

patches.title: resolve_btfids: Emit bpf_kfunc BTF decl tag for discovered kfuncs
patches.author: Ihor Solodrai
patches.summary: Implements the core emission logic in resolve_btfids: after discovering kfuncs from BTF ID sets, the tool now writes a "bpf_kfunc" decl tag into the BTF blob for each identified function. This makes kfunc identity introspectable at runtime without relying on separate pahole post-processing. Companion patches (08-11/14) extend the mechanism to also emit decl tags for KF_FASTCALL and KF_ARENA_* flagged kfuncs, and patch 13/14 removes the now-redundant pahole flags from kbuild.

patches.title: bpf: Replace scratch PTE atomically when allocating arena pages
patches.author: Tejun Heo
patches.summary: Fixes a concurrency bug in BPF arena page allocation where the scratch PTE used during page mapping was not replaced atomically, creating a window where a concurrent CPU could observe an inconsistent PTE state. The patch switches to an atomic cmpxchg-based replacement to close the race. Arena memory is increasingly used by production BPF programs (e.g., in scheduling and networking), so correctness of its page-fault path is important for reliability under load.

patches.title: bpf: Reject exclusive maps as inner maps in map-in-map
patches.author: Daniel Borkmann
patches.summary: First patch of Daniel Borkmann's 7-patch v2 series "More gen_loader fixes," which hardens the signed BPF loader (gen_loader) against misuse. This particular patch prevents exclusive maps—maps that must not be shared across programs—from being embedded as inner maps in map-in-map structures, which could bypass the exclusivity invariant. The series also fixes libbpf to skip max_entries and initial_value overrides when operating on a signed loader, and drops a redundant hash_buf from the map_get_hash operation.
